entry.title: Cloud ALM: Clarification on Access groups auto provisioning in BPMON application
entry.content:
Hi Team,

Currently, we are enabling BPMON functionality in Cloud ALM for business processes monitoring.

As we all know, we must follow two-layer security model to access business KPIs. One is at BTP subaccount level to provision role collections for restricting  and second is at BPMON application level to provision access groups.

Our concern is about automate access provisioning. Role collections provisioning, we can automate by using CIS User groups or APIs .

But we would need assistance on how to automate “Access Groups” provisioning to users in BPMON application. As we wanted to monitor business data based on legal entity(Organizational values) and will be creating Access groups for each Org. values based on KPIs, We should required to have feasibility for creating or assigning BPMON Access Groups.

We came to know that SAP does not currently provide a method/tools/options OR official public APIs for creating or assigning BPMON Access Groups.

Thus, please check and assist us how can we automate access groups provisioning.

Regards

Madhu

entry.title: Re: negative values for weight and fees in "Analyze Repor...
entry.content:
Maybe you have more returns than sales for that product.

As shown in the screenshot from your report configuration, some delivery document types are treated as subtractive, while others are treated as additive.

If you have more subtractive than additive for a given product you will see negative value in Analyze Reporting Data

entry.title: Re: France: Register test participant ID with SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition
entry.content:
Hi all,

We are currently setting up eInvoicing for France via PEPPOL.

We have successfully created the participant in DRC using the Participant ID Scheme 0225 together with the test SIREN number provided by Chorus. The participant has been activated, so everything looks fine on the DRC side.

However, our concern is how this is linked to SAP. We do not have 0225 available as a Party ID Type in our S/4HANA system. Therefore, we have so far maintained the SIREN number in all relevant master data and configuration tables (Business Partner, etc.).

When we submit an invoice from the eDocument Cockpit, we receive the following error:

If, instead, we maintain the identifier using SIRENE in all relevant tables, we receive this error:: 

 


Could you please clarify which Party ID Type should be maintained in S/4HANA for France PEPPOL? Should the Party ID Type be 0225, SIREN, SIRENE, or something else? How is the Participant ID Scheme (0225) mapped to the Party ID maintained in SAP?

entry.title: What's New in SAP Integrated Product Development: May 2026
entry.content:
What’s New for SAP Integrated Product Development

Welcome to the May 2026 blog! This update introduces powerful enhancements designed to accelerate innovation, strengthen collaboration, and improve efficiency across the product lifecycle. With expanded engineering capabilities and deeper integration with SAP and partner solutions, it further enhances how teams work together—from concept through manufacturing and beyond.

Change Management

Default Status Profiles for Change Requests and Change Implementations 

As part of the standard shipment, predefined status profiles are provided for the Change Request and Change Implementation sections, each containing a set of predefined statuses.

 [Shows an overview of the changes and UI, as outlined in the text.]

To learn more about the feature, see Managing Statuses for Change Management.

Updates to Relevance and Implementation Action in Change Management Apps

The Change Management apps are now available to simplify and streamline the user experience.

Key enhancements include:
• Removal of the “Default Relevance” column from the Configure Change Process Types app
• Removal of the “Implementation Action” column from the Change Request and Change Implementation tabs.
• Removal of the “Relevance” column from the Change Implementation section in the Manage Change Process app
• Updated “Relevance” values in the Change Request section of the Manage Change Process app:

  • New Version
  • Reference
  • Not Relevant
  • Changebbg

[Shows an overview of the changes and UI, as outlined in the text.]

To learn more about the feature, see Managing Change Validities, Problem Reports, and Change Requests.

Default Status Profiles in Configure Change Process Types

The Configure Change Process Types app now includes the new default status profiles: DFLT_CR_SP and DFLT_CI_SP which are automatically preselected for change request and change implementation processes and can no longer be modified.

[Shows an overview of the changes and UI, as outlined in the text.]

To learn more about the feature, see Configuring Change Process Types.

Common Functions 

Authorization contexts for test management 

 Our Test Management SaaS application now allows administrators to manage access restrictions for users and teams directly within the Manage Authorization Contexts app.

[Shows an overview of the changes and UI, as outlined in the text.]

 New app for managing currency exchange rates

A new app, Manage Currency Exchange Rates, is introduced as an administrative application for managing predefined currency exchange rates with Excel import capabilities and automated timeline calculations. You can now maintain consistent, controlled exchange rates for business planning while avoiding real-time rate fluctuations.

To learn more about the feature, see Managing Currency Exchange Rates.

Authorization contexts for design items 

Our Product Data Integration SaaS application now allows administrators to manage access restrictions for users and teams directly within the Manage Authorization Contexts app.

 [Shows an overview of the changes and UI, as outlined in the text.]

To learn more about the feature, see Managing Authorization Contexts.

Formulation

 New calculation type: Estimated Price

  • Administrators can now configure calculations using the new Estimated Price calculation type, which allows product developers to estimate price for a certain primary output. The price is determined based on manually defined unit prices for the formula items.
  • A new Currencies card is now available in the Synchronize Data for Formulation app, allowing administrators to upload currency data. This data is required for maintaining price calculations.

[Shows an overview of the changes and UI, as outlined in the text.]

To learn more about the feature, see Configuring Recipe Calculations.

Support for explosion recipes in formulas

The system now automatically assigns explosion recipes to formula input items. A new Explosion Recipe column is available in the formula table to display these assignments. This column is hidden by default and can be added through personalization settings.

When the system explodes these recipes, the ingredient list is extended with items from the assigned explosion recipes. This provides a complete and accurate list of all ingredients and their percentages required to produce the primary output of the start recipe.

To enable ingredient calculations that include explosion results, administrators must configure a recipe calculation using the new Ingredients (With Explosion) calculation type.

[Shows an overview of the changes and UI, as outlined in the text.]

To learn more about the feature, see Using Explosion Recipes.

 New personalization settings for Compare Recipes app

 Users now have more control over how comparison results are displayed in the Compare Recipes app.

  • You can now choose which columns to show in the comparison table by using the personalization settings.
  • A new Comparison Results column has been added. It shows both the comparison result definition and a visual indicator. The column is hidden by default.
  • You can also reorder columns in the comparison table by dragging them to the required position.

 

[Shows an overview of the changes and UI, as outlined in the text.]

To learn more about the feature, see Comparing Recipes.

Enhanced access via OAuth2 client credentials for the Formulation APIs

 The OAuth2 Client Credentials flow has been expanded to include create and update operations alongside read operations, improving integration and data management workflows.

To learn more about the feature, see Using Formulation APIs.

 Specification Management 

Specification Management hybrid scenario ready with compositions 

The hybrid specification management scenario is now complete with users being able to load and transfer composition property values between specifications in SAP Integrated Product Development and their SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition system. This needs configuration and loading of such composition properties by your administrator, and also the implementation of SAP Note 3695812.

 [Shows an overview of the changes and UI, as outlined in the text.]

To learn more about the feature, see Integrating with SAP S/4HANA Specification Management.

Ability to select and add specification versions to multiple composition items together

This feature allows you to select multiple specification version-based composition items at once (including a Select All option) when adding them to a specification's composition table.

To learn more about the feature, see 

CAD Adapters

SAP Integrated Product Development now supports direct integration with leading MCAD authoring tools. This marks a significant milestone for product-centric industries, closing a critical gap between engineering design environments and enterprise product data management.

To learn more about the feature, see CAD Interfaces Now Available in SAP Integrated Product Development.

More Information / Additional Links 

Also, check out our What’s New document for regular updates!

To learn more about the features of SAP Integrated Product Development, visit the SAP Supply Chain Management Enablement Hub.

You are invited to join the  PLM Experts Forum group on LinkedIn.

By the way, do you know how to provide feedback for SAP Integrated Product Development? Watch this video to find out how and send your feedback directly from the application. We always look forward to hearing from you.

entry.title: SAP Signavio Journey Modeler : A Practical Guide to Customer Journey Map and Journey Model
entry.content:
This blog outlines a well thought-out SAP Signavio Customer Journey Modeling functionality. A Journey Model illustrates an "Outside-In view" of the organization. This "Outside-In view" perspective can be of  a customer, Supplier, Partner, Applicant , or any other person interacting with the organization. For demonstration purpose, I have chosen the Customer Journey Map and Journey Model.

If you want to gain a deep understanding of SAP Signavio - Process Manager & Process Collaboration Hub, check out my blog post link below:

  • The nuts and bolts of a well-thought-out SAP Signavio : Process Manager & Process Collaboration Hub. 

SAP Signavio offers two ways to create Customer Journeys. One refers to design. The other refers to model a table-based Customer Journey.

  • Table-Based Customer Journey uses dedicated SAP Signavio Journey Modeler.
  • Design-Based Customer Journey uses the notation for Customer Journey Map.

Why Organizations need Journey Modeler?

Every customer Interacts differently with the organizations. Customer experience has a tremendous impact on organization`s business decisions. What it all boils down to is a "customer experience". If companies are in global competition, customer experience must be managed, so that it can be constantly improved. If customer gets a positive experience, it certainly enhances the organization`s Goodwill, and eventually increases organization`s sales and revenues.

What If customer gets bad experience, and organization turns blind eye?. What if organizations focus only on the smooth execution of their internal processes rather than looking at the things from customer point of view.

How can customer experiences be improved? 

Customer Journey Model(CJM) is the best way to improve customer experience. Customer Journey Model provides a new perspective. It allows organizations to see them from "Outside-In". Instead of focusing on internal operations, the focus shifts to the results of those operations and how the customer perceives the process. A journey model highlights the steps involved between the customer and the organization interactions from the customer perspective. These interactions happen as part of the journey of the customer experience to get a product or service.

My Business Use Case:

My Organization supplies Coffee Beans and Coffee Powder to coffee shop owners. I have leveraged SAP Signavio Journey Modeler to analyze and improve customer expectations across the end-to-end journey.

My Customers (Coffee Shop Owners) are as follows:

I will begin by explaining the customer Journey Map and then move on to Customer Journey Modeling(CJM).

When to Use a Journey Map and Journey Model:

Journey Map: Use customer Journey Map when

  • We want a high-level visualization of customer experiences.
  • We conduct brainstorming sessions.
  • We need executive-level storytelling.

Journey Model: Use customer Journey Model when

  • We need detailed Journey analysis.
  • We want to capture personas, touchpoints, sentiments, IT systems, and metrics.
  • We want to connect Journeys with business processes.
  • We are performing customer experience initiatives, process improvement.

Customer Journey Map:

The customer journey map is the visualization of the steps of customer interactions throughout customer journey with the organization, from discovery , to purchase , to post-purchase, and to re-order. A story based representation of how customer interacts with the organization. It focuses on:

  • Customer Actions
  • Emotions
  • Touchpoints
  • Experience
  • Pain points
  • Moment Of Truth

Customer Journey Map starts with the customer. Let us first create a persona.

Before we model customer journey map, we need to understand customers. Personas are widely adopted concept for breaking down quantitative customer data into customer archetypes that are easier to communicate and relate to. The characteristics of the personas define their customer journeys, each customer journey map should have exactly one persona. This persona should represent a relevant customer segment.

In SAP Signavio Process Modeler, we can add persona as a graphical element to a customer journey map and highlight key ideas, goals, and demands as style banners.

As part of this demonstration, since my customers belong to the same location, and they have had similar goals, expectations, and pain points, I have created two personas representing my customer segment. They run their own coffee shops, and buy coffee beans and coffee powder directly from my organization.

 

Mini Note: We can have current state customer journey map and future state customer journey map. In the future state journey map, we again identify the touch points with the business process landscape and compare with the ones identified in current state customer journey. From the result of the comparison, we can form the action items that become basis for the future state process landscape.

Map the "Current State"  Customer Journey

Now, as we know who our customers are and what they want , we start mapping out the customer journey. Each customer journey is a sequence of the core elements steps, touch points and moments of truth.

  • Steps are connected via simple lines and represents the sequence of events at high-level. At each step, the customers interact with the organization to have more product/service information, make decisions, or encounter a challenge.
  • Touchpoints represent steps where customer comes into direct contact, and each touch point relates to at least one of the business processes or IT systems.
  • Moment of Truth is the key decision point that can make or break organization`s business chance for succeeding with the customer. In essence, they are barriers requiring customer decision.

 

Let us choose the folder to place the Customer Journey Map.

Now, let us provide the title for Customer Journey Map as "Coffee Supply Customer Journey (AS-IS)" and start creating "Personas" and "Banners" to define our Customer Journey.

Let us connect the "Dictionary Objects" to elements of the Customer Journey Map.

Coffee Supply Customer Journey (AS-IS)

In my example scenario, the customer segment is irritated that my business doesn`t go the extra mile to make their path smooth to complete the end to end process. They have to follow up with sales rep for order confirmations , quality defects, and reorder request.

 

Let us now map the "Future State" customer journey.

Since we have known who our customers are, and what they want, we can start mapping out the customer journey. Each Customer Journey is a sequence of the core elements steps, touch points, and moments of truth.

Coffee Supply Customer Journey (To-Be)

 

Let us now publish Customer Journey Maps. 

 Post publish, we can mark the Customer Journey Maps as "Favorites" and view them on "Home" page.

 

Customer Journey Modeling(CJM): 

SAP Signavio Journey Modeler captures journeys in a tabular structure and connects what customers are thinking with the internal processes and analytics. In essence, it is a structured data-driven version of the Journey Map. It links processes and systems.

When we create a customer journey model, we depict an "outside-in view" of the organization.  While the business processes are best expressed in a diagram and mapped with BPMN, the experience of people on the receiving end of business processes is best expressed as a journey. With SAP Signavio Journey Modeler, we can capture customer journey in a tabular structure.

As part of this blog scenario, we explore various functionalities within the journey modeler:

  • Create a Persona within the dictionary
  • Create a Journey Model
  • Stages and Steps
  • Touchpoints & Text (Experience)
  • Sentiments
  • Link Business Process
  • Journey Complexity Score
  • Journey Model Dimensions

Persona within the Dictionary: 

Create a Journey Model:

 

Let us name the customer journey model as "Coffee Shop Owner Journey(Domestic).

let us select the folder for the customer journey model "Coffee Shop Owner Journey(Domestic)" to place in the Collaboration Hub.

Now, add the description for the Customer Journey Model "Coffee Shop Owner Journey(Domestic)" under the header area.

 

let us now link the Journey Owners/Personas/Journey Modelers

 

 

Define Stages and Steps:

The top row of the tabular journey model depicts the stages of a journey. The different stages represent points in the timeline. Each stage can contain multiple columns, commonly referred to as steps.

Now, let us add the stages and steps for our customer journey model.

 

Touchpoints & Text:

Touchpoints are points of contact between the customer and organization or product. We can use the option "Add Section" to add new sections. For Instance, let us add "Touch Points" and "Text" to update customer experience throughout the journey.

Text can be used to track customer experience at each step throughout the Customer Journey.

 

 

Sentiments:

This step is all about identifying the emotions of the customer. Each step triggers an emotion for the customer. The emotion can be positive, neutral, or negative. This emotion graph is used to measure the emotional level of the customer across the journey Customer Journey Model. This helps organization understand at which step customer is satisfied, or needs assistance, or is open to interaction. 

 

Link Business Processes:

We can link business processes diagrams for each step of the customer journey.

 

 

Metrics for Complexity Score: 

Complexity Score helps organization measure customer journey maturity. The perspective of someone interacting with the organization is expressed in a journey model. The complexity score gives an additional data point to evaluate customer journey models. It reflects the total operational complexity from linked BPMN processes and journeys.

With the complexity score, organizations can get an impression how challenging it is to complete the journey. The customer experience is improved by reducing the complexity of journeys and by making underlying processes more efficient.

We can use the following Metric Types for Customer Journey Model:

  • Net Promoter Score (NPS) : Customers holding positive, neutral, or negative feelings about the product or service.
  • Customer Effort Score (CES) : Customers rating  product or service on a scale from easy to difficult.
  • Customer Satisfaction Score (CSAT) : Customers rating their experience with the product or service on a scale from satisfied to dissatisfied.

We can add the metrics manually, or automatically with "Qualtrics" or "SAP Signavio API". For this scenario, I  have chosen manual option.

 

 

 

Journey Model Dimensions:

Journey Model Dimensions provide an at-a glance overview of the size of the journey model(grid size), and the number of nonempty fields. Detailed counts, for example, the number of linked personas and images are also provided.

Detail View panel shows detailed information in two parts. In the Structure Part, we can view the total number of stages, columns, and rows. The Content Part shows the different sections types in the Journey Model with their number.

For Linked Processes, each process is only counted once even the same process is linked to several cells. 

 

Now, let us preview and publish the customer journey model "Coffee Shop Owner Journey(Domestic)".

 

 

Once the Customer Journey Model is published, we can view the model under "Published" workspace view within the Collaboration Hub.

 

We can make use of the published Journey Model for future Journey Models when we save it as a template.

 

Let us enter the Template Name and activate the check box "Clear Content". The clear content option is to create a template with the same formatting of the current Journey Model.

Let us select a folder.

We must publish the template to create a new Journey Model from it.

Once it is published, we can view the newly published template under the Journey Model Templates tab.

 

Lessons Learned:

  • Customer pain points often become visible only when processes are viewed from customer`s perspective.
  • Departments may believe their internal processes are efficient, yet customers can still experience delays and frustration.
  • SAP Signavio Journey Models create a common language between business, process owners, and customer experience teams.
  • Sentiment, Moment Of Truth, and Touchpoints provide valuable context that traditional process models can not capture.
  • IT systems should be linked to journey stages to identify where technology improvements can positively impact customer experience.
  • Future-State Journey Modeling helps organizations prioritize transformation initiatives based on customer value rather than process convenience.

Conclusion:

SAP Signavio Customer Journey Modeling(CJM) provides the organization with the customer perspective on the business process landscape. By creating a Customer Journey Map and Model in SAP Signavio, organizations can connect Customer Journeys with internal business processes and have the basic foundation for optimizing customer experience.

😊This brings me to the end of process walk-through of SAP Signavio Customer Journey Map and Customer Journey Modeler. I hope this blog helps process teams understand high-level aspect of SAP Signavio Customer Journey Modeling (CJM) functionalities.

 

--

Yours Sincerely,

Pardhasaradhi Reddy. C

entry.title: Re: Regarding unexpected behavior in batch input for ME12
entry.content:
Here are my two cents on the matter:

  1. Ensure the add-on program fully refreshes or reloads vendor data before processing each record during mass updates.
    (Prevents stale data from affecting transactions.)

  2. Verify that no active sessions or unsaved changes exist in transaction ME12 before initiating mass maintenance.
    (Avoids conflicts with ongoing user activities.)

  3. Validate vendor master data for inconsistencies or special characters that may cause processing errors.
    (Ensures data integrity before execution.)

  4. Consider executing mass maintenance in separate batches per vendor group to isolate issues.
    (Improves error identification and minimizes disruption.)

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4 HANA Public Cloud: Inbound Delivery Printing...
entry.content:
Dear @Ley,

Based on SAP documentation there is no standard PDF output / print preview for the Inbound Delivery document itself in Public Cloud, unlike Sales Orders or Purchase Orders. The message No possible entries exist for this field when selecting an Output Type is therefore consistent with the standard limitation. SAP confirms that output for inbound delivery is currently not supported in the Cloud environment

More details below:

SAP KBA 3345360 - Output is not generated for Inbound Delivery

As a workaround, the output can be handled from the Goods Movement / Material Document side after the relevant goods movement is posted, not from the inbound delivery document itself. SAP KBA 3669809 explains that when goods movements are posted from areas such as Change Inbound Delivery / VL32N, output may not be generated automatically on the corresponding material document unless the relevant Output Parameter Determination settings are maintained for Goods Movement output for example GOODS_ISSUE_SLIP or GOODS_RECEIPT_PO_SLIP

More details below:

SAP KBA 3669809 - Goods receipt output missing from non inventory management apps

If the business requirement is a dedicated inbound delivery PDF form the correct approach is to submit an improvement request via SAP Customer Influence. SAP KBA 2963059 describes this as the channel for Public Cloud enhancement ideas, where customers can submit and vote for product improvements

More details below:

SAP KBA 2963059 - How to Submit Feature Requests for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: How to hide create invoice in J1IGSUBCON
entry.content:
There appears to be no available option to hide this functionality through authorization checks or via BAdI/Exit (no EXCLUDING option exists).

FORM set_pf_stat USING p_lv_stat.
  IF mod_cre = 'X'.
    SET PF-STATUS 'J_1IG_SUB'.
  ELSEIF mod_exp EQ 'X'.                                    "2600365
    SET PF-STATUS 'J_1IG_SUBEXP'.                           "2600365
  ELSE.
    SET PF-STATUS 'STANDARD'.
  ENDIF.
ENDFORM.                    " SET_PF_STAT

To address this requirement, you may adjust the GUI status via transaction SHD0.

entry.title: Re: 🐍 July Developer Challenge Week 1 — Model the Board
entry.content:
schema.cds

namespace snakeladder;

// ── Status enum — the heart of the state machine ──────────────────────────
type TurnStatus : String enum {
  Waiting;    // Session created, not yet started
  Playing;    // It's this player's turn — roll the dice!
  Moving;     // Dice rolled, player token is being moved
  Blocked;    // Player hit a double-headed snake — skips next turn
  ExtraTurn;  // Player rolled a six — gets another turn
  AFK;        // Player has been idle for too long — skips next turn
  Finished;   // This player has reached square 100 and won
}

// ── Board square — pre-loaded with snake/ladder data ──────────────────────
entity BoardSquares {
  key square      : Integer;        // 1–100
      snakeTo     : Integer;        // 0 if no snake on this square
      ladderTo    : Integer;        // 0 if no ladder on this square
      isDoubleHead: Boolean default false;  // true if this is a double-headed snake mouth
}

// ── Game session ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
entity GameSessions {
  key ID          : UUID;
      name        : String(100);    // e.g. "Friday Afternoon Game"
      createdAt   : Timestamp;
      startedAt   : Timestamp;
      finishedAt  : Timestamp;
      winner      : Association to Players;
}

// ── Player — one row per player per session ───────────────────────────────
@flow.status: TurnStatus
entity Players {
  key ID          : UUID;
      session     : Association to GameSessions;
      name        : String(100);
      @readonly
      position    : Integer default 1;      // current square (1–100)
      @readonly
      TurnStatus  : TurnStatus default #Waiting;
      turnOrder   : Integer;                // 1 = goes first
      lastRoll    : Integer;               // last dice result
}

game-service.cds

using { snakeladder } from '../db/schema'; 

service GameService {

  entity GameSessions as projection on snakeladder.GameSessions;

  @flow.status: TurnStatus
  entity Players as projection on snakeladder.Players actions {

    // Week 2 will implement these — declare them now so the model is complete
    @from: [ #Waiting ]
    @TO: #Playing
    action startTurn();

    @from: [ #Playing ]
    @TO: #Moving
    action rollDice() returns Integer;

    @from: [ #Moving ]
    @TO: #Waiting                  // transitions to next player in Week 3
    action confirmMove();

    @from: [ #Moving ]
    @TO: #Blocked                  // double-headed snake bite
    action blockPlayer();

    @from: [ #Blocked ]
    @TO: $flow.previous            // restores pre-block status
    action unblockPlayer();

    @from: [ #Moving ]
    @TO: #Finished
    action winGame();

    /*** Additional Actions ***/
    @from: [ #ExtraTurn ]
    @TO: #Moving
    action rollAgain() returns Integer;

    @from: [ #Waiting, #Playing ]
    @TO: #AFK
    action setPlayerInactive ();

    @from: [ #AFK ]
    @TO: #Waiting
    action IAmBack();
  }

  entity BoardSquares as projection on snakeladder.BoardSquares;
}

I extended the TurnStatus enum with ExtraTurn (for bonus rolls) and AFK to handle player inactivity in a multi-player setup.

entry.title: Re: Moving HTML5 Applications to Application Frontend Service
entry.content:
Hi Yashika

No, we are not sunsetting HTML5 repository, it will continue to be available and developed as needed. Application Frontend service brings new qualities for the HTML5 application development and operation that are not possible with HTML5 repository. While we aim Application Frontend more towards greenfield scenario, you can also easily move apps from HTML5 Repo into it, that what this blog is about. 
In any case, both services will reside side by side for the foreseeable future.

Regarding sync with SAP Build Work Zone, yes this is possible see here for more details. Currently this is a manual process, we are working to make it automatic.

I hope this helps.

entry.title: Re: 🐍 July Developer Challenge Week 1 — Model the Board
entry.content:
db/schema.cds

namespace snakeladder;

// ── Status enum — the heart of the state machine ──────────────────────────
type TurnStatus : String enum {
  Waiting;    // Session created, not yet started
  Playing;    // It's this player's turn — roll the dice!
  Moving;     // Dice rolled, player token is being moved
  Blocked;    // Player hit a double-headed snake — skips next turn
  Finished;   // This player has reached square 100 and won
}

// ── Board square — pre-loaded with snake/ladder data ──────────────────────
entity BoardSquares {
  key square      : Integer;        // 1–100
      snakeTo     : Integer;        // 0 if no snake on this square
      ladderTo    : Integer;        // 0 if no ladder on this square
      isDoubleHead: Boolean default false;  // true if this is a double-headed snake mouth
}

// ── Game session ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
entity GameSessions {
  key ID          : UUID;
      name        : String(100);    // e.g. "Friday Afternoon Game"
      createdAt   : Timestamp;
      startedAt   : Timestamp;
      finishedAt  : Timestamp;
      winner      : Association to Players;
}

// ── Player — one row per player per session ───────────────────────────────
@flow.status: TurnStatus
entity Players {
  key ID          : UUID;
      session     : Association to GameSessions;
      name        : String(100);
      @readonly
      position    : Integer default 1;      // current square (1–100)
      @readonly
      TurnStatus  : TurnStatus default #Waiting;
      turnOrder   : Integer;                // 1 = goes first
      lastRoll    : Integer;               // last dice result
}

srv/game-service.cds

using { snakeladder } from '../db/schema';

service GameService @(path: '/odata/v4/GameService') {

  entity GameSessions as projection on snakeladder.GameSessions;

  @flow.status: TurnStatus
  entity Players as projection on snakeladder.Players actions {

    // Week 2 will implement these — declare them now so the model is complete
    @from: [ #Waiting ]
    @TO: #Playing
    action startTurn();

    @from: [ #Playing ]
    @TO: #Moving
    action rollDice() returns Integer;

    @from: [ #Moving ]
    @TO: #Waiting                  // transitions to next player in Week 3
    action confirmMove();

    @from: [ #Moving ]
    @TO: #Blocked                  // double-headed snake bite
    action blockPlayer();

    @from: [ #Blocked ]
    @TO: $flow.previous            // restores pre-block status
    action unblockPlayer();

    @from: [ #Moving ]
    @TO: #Finished
    action winGame();
  }

  entity BoardSquares as projection on snakeladder.BoardSquares;
}

GameService endpoints screenshot:

I have used the same TurnStatus enum values to see how the builds in coming weeks, I can model the game at later stages by adding more values.
Additionally, I have used annotation path to get the response using Ajay's path

entry.title: Re: Issue During stock posting from purchase order
entry.content: If the stock type before clicking save icon shows the correct one (INSMK = X, quality inspection) and it only get to the wrong one after saving the document, a breakpoint can be set at statement CALL FUNCTION IN UPDATE TASK, to find out where the XEKPO-INSMK is updated.

entry.title: Re: Purchase order creation with a date that lies in the...
entry.content: The document date is proposed with the system date by default, but it can be manually changed before saving the document. To prevent the manual change to a future date, BAdI ME_PROCESS_PO_CUST can be used to add the custom check and issue a custom error.

entry.title: Re: Error in changes purchase order
entry.content:
Transaction SM13 can be used to view the details of the update termination error. It will show the function name where the error happens and the error message details.

To find out what data is imported and causes the error, you may set a breakpoint at the statement CALL FUNCTION IN UPDATE TASK and change the PO to reproduce the error. When you stop at the breakpoint which calls the function module, you can check the import data and verify which entry / field is wrong.

entry.title: Re: SAP Career Roadmap for Build Execution team
entry.content:
Hi   
If you already  strong foundation in SAP technologies and cloud environments through your hands-on experience with SAP landscape provisioning and operations.you might want to consider at this stage of your career on building broad expertise while exploring the areas that interest you most. There is no single "best" career path—SAP offers many opportunities across platform technologies, cloud operations, security, architecture, automation, and business solutions.

In addition to areas such as SAP BTP, Security & GRC, and SAP Cloud ALM, I would strongly encourage you to invest time in AI-related capabilities. AI is becoming a key part of the SAP ecosystem and is increasingly embedded in application development, automation, integration, analytics, and business processes through SAP BTP and Joule. Understanding how AI, automation, and business data work together will be valuable regardless of the specialization you choose.

Continuing gaining hands-on experience, follow relevant learning journeys, pursue certifications where appropriate, and stay curious about emerging technologies will be helpful as well.. A strong technical foundation combined with knowledge of cloud, automation, and AI will keep many career options open—from specialist and architect roles to future leadership positions.

For learning options, I recommend exploring the learn by product sites on  https://learning.sap.com/

  • SAP Business Technology Platform where yo can learn about SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP), the platform for the Intelligent Enterprise.
  • Autonomous Enterprise to gain the expertise to lead an AI-powered digital transformation that unifies your data and scales your enterprise.
  • SAP Integration Suite to learn how to connect systems, automate business processes, and harmonize experiences across the extended enterprise.

These sites provide a structured path from fundamentals to advanced topics and can help you identify which area best matches your interests and career goal.

The SAP ecosystem offers many exciting career paths, so I encourage other community members to join the conversation and share their insights and experiences as well.

Wishing you all the best on your SAP career journey!

Best regards,
Margit Wagner

entry.title: Re: Welcome to our SAP Learning Group moderated by SAP Learning Experts
entry.content:
Hi @Poori_Ganesh

Welcome to our group and thank you to take a moment to raise your question.

As Assistance Developer Architect, you have for sure a strong foundation for moving into UX. Your experience understanding user needs, content strategy, and collaboration with product teams aligns well with many UX practices.

To get started, I'd recommend focusing on:

  • UX Fundamentals and Design Thinking
  • User Research and Usability Testing
  • Information Architecture
  • Interaction Design
  • Wireframing and Prototyping (for example, with Figma)
  • Accessibility and Inclusive Design

You may also find these SAP Learning resources helpful:

  • SAP S/4HANA UX for Business and Functional Experts
  • How to Deliver a Great User Experience with SAP S/4HANA 
  • Developing UIs with SAPUI5 

In addition, I've prepared a tailored SAP Learning search for you. Applying the following filters:

Keyword: UX
Level: Beginner
Role: Architect

This will help you discover learning journeys and courses that align with your current background while building a solid foundation in UX concepts and practices.

Best of luck on your UX journey.

Feel free to reach out and ask your questions please post questions in our  Q&A area. This makes it easier  to follow-up:

  • That way other learners can benefit from the answer
  • If you ask the question there other learners can search for it in the future if they run into the same issue and there we have more features available for responses

Kind regads
Margit

entry.title: WBS Validation Table PRTE Not Available in Boolean Class Despite VWTYGB01 and RGUGBR00 – Error GB082
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We are unable to use the WBS Basic Start Date (PRTE-PSTRT) and Basic Finish Date (PRTE-PENDE) fields in our PS Validation (OPSI). This is blocking us from enforcing a mandatory date validation for Level 2 WBS elements under company codes S135 and S100, which is a key data quality control requirement for our Investment project type.


Validation Configuration:
Validation Name: ZPS01 — Scania WBS Validation
Step 008 — Bsic strt & end dates are mandatory

Prerequisite:
PRPS-PRART = 'IV' AND PRPS-STUFE = '2' AND PRPS-PBUKR = 'S135' AND PRPS-PBUKR = 'S100'

Check:
PRTE-PSTRT <> ' ' AND PRTE-PENDE <> ' '

Message: ZFI_M 106 — Error (Invalid logical system) (This message is just for the testing purpose).

Steps Performed to Make PRTE Available:
By default, the WBS date fields PRTE-PSTRT (Basic Start Date) and PRTE-PENDE (Basic Finish Date) are not available in the Validation & Substitution Boolean class structure pool for PS WBS Elements. To make them available, the following two steps were pe

entry.title: Parloa + SAP Service Cloud V2: Building a Voice AI Agent for Automated Service Request Management
entry.content:
In this blog, we explore how Parloa AI Agent Management Platform can be integrated with SAP Service Cloud Version 2 to automate a common customer service scenario: Service Request Status Inquiry and Update.

By combining Parloa's conversational Voice AI capabilities with SAP Service Cloud V2's service management foundation, organizations can deliver a seamless voice-first customer experience.

https://www.parloa.com/sap/  

Business Scenario

Imagine a manufacturing company that receives hundreds of customer calls every day asking questions such as:

  • What is the status of my service request?
  • When will my technician arrive?
  • Has my replacement product been shipped?
  • Can I update my contact number?

These calls are repetitive and consume a significant portion of support agent capacity.

Instead of waiting for an agent, customers can interact with an AI-powered voice assistant that:

  1. Understands customer requests.
  2. Authenticates the caller.
  3. Retrieves service request information from SAP Service Cloud V2.
  4. Provides status updates in natural language.
  5. Updates customer information when required.
  6. Escalates to a human agent when necessary.
+---------------------+
| Customer Phone Call |
+----------+----------+
           |
           v
+---------------------+
|      Parloa AI      |
| Voice Conversation  |
+----------+----------+
           |
           | REST APIs
           v
+---------------------+
| CTI / Middleware  |
+----------+----------+
           |
           |
           v
+---------------------+
| SAP Service Cloud V2|
| Service Requests    |
| Customers           |
| Cases               |
+----------+----------+
           |
           v
+---------------------+
| Service Agents      |
+---------------------+

Why Parloa?

Parloa provides enterprise-grade AI voice agents that can:

  • Understand natural language conversations
  • Handle multi-turn dialogues
  • Support omnichannel engagement
  • Integrate with backend business applications
  • Transfer conversations to human agents
  • Provide real-time conversation orchestration

For enterprises already running SAP Service Cloud V2, Parloa becomes the conversational front-end while SAP remains the system of record.

SAP Service Cloud V2 as the Service Backbone

SAP Service Cloud V2 offers:

  • Case Management
  • Service Requests
  • Installed Base and Registered Products
  • Customer Master Data
  • Knowledge Base
  • Service Quotes
  • Service Analytics

Parloa x SAP One Pager

 

Future Enhancements

Once the basic integration is established, organizations can extend the Voice AI agent to:

  • Create new service tickets
  • Schedule technician visits
  • Identify installed products
  • Provide warranty information
  • Trigger field service activities
  • Recommend knowledge base articles
  • Perform sentiment analysis
  • Integrate with Joule and SAP Business AI services

More details will updated soon

entry.title: Code Connect 2026: Final Info Before We Kick Off!
entry.content:
Code Connect 2026 is just around the corner! From July 13–16, 2026, the SAP developer community will gather in St. Leon-Rot, Germany – onsite and online.

Here’s everything you need to know before we get started.

 

 

We Are Fully Booked!

All onsite tickets are fully booked - thank you for the amazing interest!

If you couldn’t secure a ticket:

  • Join the waiting lists on Code Connect 2026 in case seats become available.
  • Follow selected sessions via live stream or Microsoft Teams link.

Even if you're not onsite, you can still be part of Code Connect. Selected sessions will be streamed live, and recordings of some sessions will be available afterwards. Check back soon for details.

 

 

 

UI5con (July 14)

  • Main stage sessions will be live streamed on YouTube.
  • Check the agenda to see which sessions are streamed.

 

 

re>≡CAP (July 15)

  • Main stage sessions will be broadcasted.
  • Some sidetracks (W1/W2) can be attended via Microsoft Teams link.
  • Check the agenda to see which sessions are broadcasted or available via Microsoft Teams link.

 

 

HANA Tech Con (July 16)

  • The links will be announced on the event day.

 

The Agendas Are Live

The full program is already available - time to plan your schedule!

Check-In opens at 8.00 AM every day. Please bring the QR code with you that you can find in your ticket.

July 13 – Code Jams & Community Meetup

  • Hands-on Code Jams:
    • OpenUI5
    • CAP
    • AI Agents
  • Informal pre-event meetup (no registration needed):
    Ihle Besen, Höfe am Sträßel 3, 69231 Rauenberg
    5:00 PM CEST

July 14–16 – Main Conference Days

Explore all agendas:

  • July 14: UI5con
  • July 15: re>≡CAP
  • July 16: HANA Tech Con

On July 14 also the HANA AI CodeJam takes place.

 

Location

Code Connect takes place at:

SAP-Allee 27
68789 St. Leon-Rot, Germany

Find detailed information on directions, parking, and public transport here.

 

Final Tips

  • Bookmark your favorite sessions in advance
  • Arrive early for popular sessions (rooms fill quickly!)
  • Bring your laptop for Code Jams and hands-on sessions/ workshops
  • Don’t miss the networking moments - some of the best conversations happen in between sessions
  • Follow the agendas closely for last-minute updates

 

See You Soon!

We’re looking forward to an inspiring week of learning, sharing, and connecting.

See you at Code Connect 2026!

entry.title: Matchning incoming payment automatically without leading zero
entry.content:
Hi, 

We are trying to create rules so incoming payments can be matched automatically in the Bank statement. 

The problem is that in the Customer Line items we have a field that starts with zero, and the customer pays without the zeros. 
Example 000000672026REPP but the customer send us this reference CINV 672026REPP. 

I have read and found that we should use Interpretation Algorithms 026 "Search for reference document number with leading zero, if <10". I have connected this algorithm to external transaction that I have added to on the bank statement row. 

But I don´t get it work, because on the rule I can´t choose this algorithm. How can I solve this?

I 

 

 

 

/Olivia

entry.title: Re: New Employee Business Partner Data Model in SAP S/4HANA 2020 On-Premise
entry.content:
Hi,
Reports /SHCM/RH_SYNC_BUPA_EMPL_SINGLE  and /SHCM/RH_SYNC_BUPA_FROM_EMPL must run without errors and must create both employee-BP and employment-BP. 
Then run report /SHCM/R_EMPL_HANDLE_BPROLES (TR /SHCM/BPROLE_SYNC) respective in background /SHCM/R_EMPL_HDLE_BPRLES_DELTA (TR /SHCM/BPROLE_SYNCJOB). Please check that both bank data and address data must exist at employee for vendor role creation.

Please also note this SAP note:
2578294 - FAQ - Employee to Business Partner synchronization in S/4HANA OnPremise

BR
Dieter

entry.title: Re: When was the last time that you upgraded your supply chain planning system?
entry.content:
Great Blog on the key capabilities of SAP IBP!  

And if you would like to enhance your planning process even more by adding the supplier dimension, have a look into SAP Business Network Planning Collaboration . Forecasts and demand plans from IBP are then shared directly with trading partners. Supplier commitments, inventory levels, and deviations flow back in real time.

Emails to employees are always sent to their Business Email. Only option I see: the users have to add their Private Email to email type business. But I would never recommend this or sending Business Emails from EC to a private Email address.

entry.title: Is Your Payment Stack Ready for the Holiday Rush?
entry.content:
What is the Open Payment Framework (OPF)?

OPF is a low-code payment integration platform built directly into SAP Commerce Cloud. It addresses a long-standing challenge for SAP merchants: traditionally, integrating payment service providers (PSPs) required custom development, code extensions, and ongoing maintenance tied to Commerce releases.

OPF removes that dependency. Merchants and partners can configure PSP integrations through a dedicated workbench, no custom code to write, deploy, or maintain. Payment integration becomes a configuration task rather than a development project.

OPF Key Capabilities

  • No additional cost: OPF is available to all SAP Commerce Cloud customers as part of their existing contract.
  • Faster time to value: PSP integrations can be configured and deployed significantly faster than traditional custom development, reducing go-live timelines and dependency on development resources.
  • Lower total cost of ownership: no custom code means no maintenance burden when SAP Commerce Cloud is updated.
  • Flexible payment flows: OPF supports authorization, capture, refund, cancellation, and notifications, as well as front-end patterns including iFrame, hosted pages, and hosted fields.
  • Payment partner ecosystem, ready to activate: integrations with PayPal and Checkout.com partnership are available without writing a line of code. Also, OPF supports APMs, Apple Pay, Google Pay.
  • Merchant visibility and control: built-in logging, transaction visibility, and manual status overrides for customer service agents.
  • B2B ready: OPF supports specific flows such as manual and account-based payments. Merchants running B2B storefronts on SAP Commerce Cloud can enforce flexible organization controls, and establish governance capabilities that reduce operational risk, staying operationally lean.

Want to learn more? Join our dedicated Webcast:

Webcast on  July 9, 2026

Getting Holiday-Ready with SAP Commerce Cloud Payments: Unlocking Peak Season Success with Open Payment Framework and Leading Payment Partners

Thursday, 9 July 2026  |  4:00 PM CEST  |  1hour 15 minutes  |  Online – Live

The session covers SAP Commerce Cloud's payment strategy and a hands-on look at OPF  how it enables agility and provider flexibility at scale. You will also hear directly from two of SAP's newest OPF partners, PayPal and Checkout.com:

  • insights on authorization rate optimization and building a resilient payment setup for high-traffic periods.
  • a concrete example of OPF's value in practice: merchants on SAP Commerce Cloud can activate a payment method without any custom development.

Speakers:
Fagner Santos: SAP Lead Product Manager
Featuring expert insights from industry leaders at PayPal and Checkout.com 

Register here

Resources

    • Training- OPF Microlearning Videos
    • Onboarding guide
    • Help center documentation
    • Demo Video - SAP Commerce Cloud, open payment framework Overview - SAP Media Share
    • SAP Commerce Cloud, Open Payment Framework | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Advance Holiday Pay (AHP) UK
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

We are currently going through a UK SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll (ECP) implementation and are looking for some guidance from other customers.

One of our payroll requirements is to pay holiday pay in advance for weekly paid employees. For example, where an employee is due to be paid for a week's holiday, the holiday pay would be paid in the payroll period before the holiday is taken.

Historically, we understood that SAP UK Payroll supported Advance Holiday Pay (AHP) functionality, but during our ECP implementation there is some uncertainty around whether this functionality is still available and supported as standard.

We're keen to hear from other customers who have addressed a similar requirement:

  • Are you using standard Advance Holiday Pay (AHP) functionality in ECP UK?
  • How are you managing the requirement to pay weekly employees in advance of a holiday period?
  • Was standard SAP functionality sufficient, or was custom development required?
  • Have you encountered any challenges during implementation or migration from SAP ECC Payroll to ECP?
  • Has SAP provided any guidance on the recommended approach for this scenario?

We're trying to understand whether this is a gap in our current design/configuration or whether other organisations have had similar experiences.

Any advice, lessons learned, or recommendations would be greatly appreciated.

Many thanks,

Stuart

why do you want to have an automatism that you as an employee will be forced to maintain a business email?

entry.title: Budget calculation based on Lookup Table in EC-integrated Compensation Template
entry.content:
Hello SAP SuccessFactors experts,

We are currently using a non-Employee Central integrated Compensation template.

Our budget is calculated based on a Lookup Table, which allows us to assign different budget percentages depending on employee-specific criteria.

Next year, we plan to migrate to an Employee Central integrated Compensation template.

I found documentation stating that budget calculations and budget assignments are supported in EC-integrated templates, but I could not find clear information regarding the use of Lookup Tables as the basis for budget calculation.

My questions are:

  1. Is it possible to continue using a Lookup Table for budget determination in an EC-integrated Compensation template?
  2. If yes, are there any limitations compared to a non-EC integrated template?

Any experiences, best practices, or references to SAP documentation would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you!

Best regards,
Joe

entry.title: MTO, quantity in SO change. How should I deal with Purchase order ?
entry.content:
My customer model is MTO. They have case as bellow

1. They already input SO into SAP, MRP run , generated Planned order and Purchase requistion

2. Purchasing department converted PR into PO already

3. Thier customer changed order quantity in SO.

4. MRP runs agian and change the requirement. However, the PR is already convered into PO so MRP can not change quantity in PO

5. Thier BOM include 100 components. Not all supplier allow to change order quantity in thier PO

In this case, how system can help the user to know which material they should change how many quantity in PO? I want it to be an report, other than system automatically changes the quantity in PO because not all supplier allow that.

Please help to suggest a solution

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: How to rename an OData V4 service?
entry.content:
You are right, I should have put "renaming" in double quotes.


Option 2 + 3 offer you the option to have a second service URL instead of having to use the original service URL but now with a changed behavior.

Which option to choose would depend on your use case. 

I you use option 1 you have to make sure to change your deployed app simultaneously because otherwise it would stop to work.

Option 3 is not "unrelated". It shows how you extend or develop further your existing service and publish the new service not only with a new URL but as a new service and it would hence require you to assign appropriate authorizations to your end users as well.

entry.title: Course Spotlight: Introducing the New SAP IBP Harmonized Planning Area Course (IBP3910)
entry.content:
Supply chain planning has never been more complex — and the tools planners use, need to match that complexity with clarity, not add to it. That's exactly what the Harmonized Planning Area (HPA) in SAP Integrated Business Planning (IBP) is designed to do. And now, there's a dedicated course to help you unlock its full potential. 

 What is the Harmonized Planning Area? 

The Harmonized Planning Area is a unified planning foundation within SAP IBP that brings together demand, supply, inventory, and response planning into a single, consistent data model. Rather than maintaining separate planning areas for each process — each with its own master data, key figures, and configurations — HPA gives planners and architects one coherent structure to work from. 

This is a significant evolution. It eliminates the data silos that have historically forced teams to reconcile key figures across disconnected planning views. When everyone plans from the same area, the numbers tell one consistent story. 

 Why Does This Matter? 

HPA unlocks a new level of planning maturity for IBP customers. By standardizing on a harmonized model, organizations can now: 

  • Reduce configuration effort — a single planning area consolidates master data, key figures, and operator setups, freeing teams to focus on planning decisions rather than maintenance overhead.
  • Accelerate S&OP alignment — demand and supply teams work from one unified data model, eliminating reconciliation friction and enabling faster, more confident consensus planning. 
  • Shorten time-to-value — new planning scenarios and use cases can be onboarded within the existing HPA structure, dramatically reducing implementation timelines. 
  • Ensure consistent data integration — RTI provides a single pipeline for TSP and OBP master data, guaranteeing consistent master data between ERP and end-to-end planning in IBP.

Together, these capabilities give cross-functional teams a shared language for planning decisions and a foundation that scales with the business — without the complexity that came with managing multiple isolated planning areas. 

 What IBP3910 Teaches — Unit by Unit 

Structured as a 2-day instructor-led training with 10 hands-on exercises, the course takes participants from foundational architecture concepts all the way through live planning runs in I_SAPIBP2. Here is what each unit covers: 

 

                                      

This isn't just conceptual. The course is designed with real- world scenarios so you leave ready to apply what you've learned on your next engagement. 

 The Business Case for Your Customers 

 For customers running SAP IBP, the value proposition is compelling: 

  • Faster planning cycles — a unified model means fewer handoffs and less time spent aligning numbers, especially between operational and tactical supply planning 
  • Lower total cost of ownership — one planning area to configure, maintain, and upgrade 
  • Greater agility — new business requirements can be modeled without rebuilding the planning foundation 
  • Better decisions — planners work from a single version of the truth, improving confidence in the numbers driving executive decisions.  

In a world where supply chains need to respond to disruptions in hours, not days, this kind of structural agility is a competitive differentiator. 

Who Is This Course For? 

IBP3910 is designed for professionals directly involved in planning, implementing, or governing SAP IBP environments: 

  • Application Consultants: Implementing or upgrading SAP IBP for clients — get ahead of HPA architecture before you hit the project. 
  • Project Stakeholders: Leading supply chain transformation programs — understand what the HPA changes and what it unlocks for your roadmap. 
  • Planning Power Users: Working daily in SAP IBP — transition confidently to I_SAPIBP2 and take advantage of unified planning workflows. 

The link to the course : 

IBP3910 - Discovering SAP IBP Harmonized Planning Area 

Happy Learning!
